item.title: The Dollar Is Riding High Thanks to a Newly Hawkish Fed
item.description: Greenback’s strength is weighing on emerging-market currencies

item.title: World Cup Mints Big Prediction Winners and Million-Dollar Losers
item.description: The World Cup is turning prediction markets into a public ledger of million-dollar bets, exposing big wins and dramatic losses as the tournament attracts more money to the fast-growing industry.

item.title: Santander Surpasses Inditex as Spain’s Most Valuable Company
item.description: Banco Santander SA has become Spain’s most valuable listed company, overtaking Zara owner Inditex SA for the first time in eight years.

item.title: Indian Banks Line Up $2.5 Billion Debt Sales Using RBI Swap
item.description: At least four lenders including the State Bank of India are preparing to raise about $2.5 billion selling bonds in the coming weeks, tapping a Reserve Bank of India facility that makes it cheaper to borrow dollars, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Stocks Draw Record Weekly Inflows as Investors Pile Into Tech
item.description: US stocks attracted record inflows last week as investors piled into the technology sector, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

item.title: Collapsed Lender MFS Said to Have Never Registered £300 Million of Mortgages
item.description: A £300 million ($396 million) portfolio of mortgages linked to collapsed lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. looks to never have been registered with public property records, according to court filings, threatening to complicate lenders’ efforts to establish their claims over the underlying assets.

item.title: South Africa Aims to Replicate Telkom’s Success With Property Firm
item.description: South Africa seeks to replicate the success achieved by bringing private partners into telecommunications firm Telkom SA SOC Ltd. with the creation of a new state company that will manage its 155-billion-rand ($9.4 billion) property portfolio.

item.title: Indian Billionaires Set for Jackpot on National Stock Exchange IPO
item.description: Some of India’s wealthiest investors stand to get even richer from their shareholdings in the nation’s stock exchange, which filed for an initial public offering this week.

item.title: Candidates Deploy Army of Poll Watchers in Colombia’s High-Stakes Election
item.description: Colombia’s presidential campaigns are both flagging the risk of fraud in Sunday’s election, and taking steps to prevent it.

item.title: Mukesh Ambani Says Jio Platforms to File IPO Papers Friday
item.description: Billionaire Mukesh Ambani said Reliance Industries Ltd.’s digital unit will submit its listing documents by Friday, starting the process of unlocking shareholder value in one of India’s most-anticipated initial public offerings.

item.title: Siemens Sees Industrial App Store Sales Doubling in Tech Push
item.description: Siemens AG expects revenue from its online store to more than double this year, though the maker of industrial software and trains is stopping short of disclosing concrete figures that would track its progress of turning into a tech-led company.

item.title: Iran War Fuels Interest in Renewables, Multilateral Lender Says
item.description: The conflict in Iran, which has sent crude prices to multi-year highs, is fueling interest in renewables and projects aimed at boosting energy security, according to the Private Infrastructure Development Group.

item.title: UK Fintech Starling Shrinks Board as McPike Cements Control
item.description: Starling Bank Ltd. is streamlining its board in light of a number of departures, a move that increases the sway of UK digital bank’s largest shareholder.

item.title: World Cup 2026’s Dynamic Pricing Has Tickets Going One Way: Up
item.description: Before the World Cup started, fans raged over the cost of tickets — the highest ever for the tournament. Critics wondered whether prices would crash before kickoff, and if swathes of empty seats would embarrass FIFA’s pricing strategy.

item.title: BOE Unveils Doomsday Scenario for Private Markets Stress Test
item.description: The Bank of England will stress test private markets against a doomsday scenario featuring 7% interest rates, a 35% collapse in UK share prices, a 400 basis-point increase in leveraged loan spreads and a wide range of artificial intelligence disruptions, the central bank announced on Friday.

item.title: Ghana Is Weighing Local Control of Gold Fields’ Biggest Mine
item.description: Ghana is considering transferring control of Gold Fields Ltd.’s Tarkwa mine to local firms when its leases expire next April, part of a push to increase control of its gold industry and capture more of the proceeds from high bullion prices. The shares plunged.

item.title: Lane Says It’s Hard to Argue ECB Shouldn’t Have Hiked Rates
item.description: It’s difficult to argue that the European Central Bank should have refrained from raising borrowing costs this month, according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Thins Friday as Shipowners Err on Side of Safety
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to thin early on Friday, just a day after a burst of oil flows through the waterway as the US and Iran vowed to lift a dual blockade.

item.title: Anthropic's Co-Founder and Top Economist on Doing Research at the AI Frontier
item.description: A conversation with Jack Clark and Peter McCrory.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Anthropic Thinks About AI Safety (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: How Anthropic Thinks About AI Safety (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: How Anthropic Thinks About AI Safety (Podcast)】

item.title: Governor Ueda Has Been Discharged From Hospital, BOJ Says
item.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda was discharged from the hospital Friday and plans to return to work on Tuesday, according to the BOJ.

item.title: Norwegian Family Offices Back $85 Million Ukraine Defense Tech Fund
item.description: A group of Norwegian investors have raised $85 million for a venture capital fund focused on Ukrainian defense technology, betting that innovations developed on the battlefield will play a central role in Europe’s security architecture.

item.title: UK Bonds Fall as Burnham Win, Oil Prices Renew Fiscal Concerns
item.description: The UK’s government bonds fell on Friday as higher oil prices renewed concerns over inflation and a win by Andy Burnham in a special election increased the country’s political uncertainty.

item.title: Malaysia Reworks Asset Rules for Officials After Azam Scandal
item.description: Malaysia has overhauled its rules for civil servants’ shareholdings and asset declarations, months after a report about the stock holdings of former anti-graft chief Azam Baki spurred public debate in the country.

item.title: Prosus Expects Surge in Profits on E-Commerce, Tencent Growth
item.description: Prosus NV expects core earnings to surge as much as 28% in the year ended March 31st on the back of its growing e-commerce business and an increase in the value of the stake it owns in China’s Tencent.

item.title: Goldman, Barclays Strategists Raise Targets for European Stocks
item.description: The US-Iran peace deal has brightened the outlook for European stocks in the second half and prompted several strategists to increase their year-end targets, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: UK Retail Sales Bounce Back in Surprising Boost for Economy
item.description: UK retail sales rebounded unexpectedly strongly in May while previous months were revised higher in a sign of consumer resilience that offers hope for the wider economy.

item.title: Record Debt Costs Show Fiscal Pressures Facing Starmer Rivals
item.description: Debt-interest costs saw UK government borrowing rise to the highest level for any May in six years, underscoring the fiscal challenges awaiting any successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: MSCI Says Low Free-Float Among Many Hurdles for Vietnam Stocks
item.description: MSCI Inc. pointed to low free-float levels at some firms and continued limits on foreign ownership as among hurdles for Vietnam stocks, potentially complicating the country’s push for a market upgrade.

item.title: Traders’ Latest AI-Related Play Is a Struggling Car Parts Stock
item.description: Investors looking for the stock market’s next artificial intelligence winner have honed in on embattled French car parts maker Valeo SE.
